Notes.Text:
This update for python-python-dotenv fixes the following issue:

- CVE-2026-28684: follow symbolic links when rewriting `.env` files (bsc#1262423)
Notes.Title: Details

Notes.Text: The CVRF data is provided by SUSE under the Creative Commons License 4.0 with Attribution (CC-BY-4.0).
Notes.Title: Terms of Use

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Python 3 15 SP7

Vulnerabilities.Description: python-dotenv reads key-value pairs from a .env file and can set them as environment variables. Prior to version 1.2.2, `set_key()` and `unset_key()` in python-dotenv follow symbolic links when rewriting `.env` files, allowing a local attacker to overwrite arbitrary files via a crafted symlink when a cross-device rename fallback is triggered. Users should upgrade to v.1.2.2 or, as a workaround, apply the patch manually.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Python 3 15 SP7:python311-python-dotenv-1.0.0-150400.9.6.1
